perturbation.original_text:
TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle a retainer fee in each Contract Year during the Contract Period ("Retainer Fee") in accordance with the following schedule:

CONTRACT YEAR RETAINER FEE
First (1st) Contract Year Fifty-Five Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1996-Dec. 31, 1996) Dollars ($55,000)
Second (2nd) Contract Year Seventy Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1997-Dec. 31, 1997) Dollars (70,000)
Third (3rd) Contract Year Ninety Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1998-Dec. 31, 1998) Dollars ($90,000)

Said Retainer Fee shall be paid in four (4) equal installments on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year.
perturbation.changed_text:
TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle a retainer fee in each Contract Year during the Contract Period ("Consideration") in accordance with the following schedule:

CONTRACT YEAR RETAINER FEE
First (1st) Contract Year Fifty-Five Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1996-Dec. 31, 1996) Dollars ($55,000)
Second (2nd) Contract Year Seventy Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1997-Dec. 31, 1997) Dollars (70,000)
Third (3rd) Contract Year Ninety Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1998-Dec. 31, 1998) Dollars ($90,000)

Said Retainer Fee shall be paid in four (4) equal installments on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the defined term 'Retainer Fee' to 'Consideration' in the introductory sentence of Paragraph 8 introduces a contradiction. The rest of the paragraph, and indeed the rest of the agreement, still refers to the 'Retainer Fee,' but the initial definition has been altered, creating uncertainty about which term to use. This will affect how a court might interpret the payment terms in the event of a dispute, especially since the term 'Retainer Fee' is used in the schedule.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
In consideration of the rights and benefits granted to TPC hereunder, TPC agrees to pay Consolidated Artists on behalf of Ogle a retainer fee in each Contract Year during the Contract Period ("Consideration") in accordance with the following schedule:

CONTRACT YEAR RETAINER FEE
First (1st) Contract Year Fifty-Five Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1996-Dec. 31, 1996) Dollars ($55,000)
Second (2nd) Contract Year Seventy Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1997-Dec. 31, 1997) Dollars (70,000)
Third (3rd) Contract Year Ninety Thousand U.S.
(Jan. 1, 1998-Dec. 31, 1998) Dollars ($90,000)

Said Retainer Fee shall be paid in four (4) equal installments on or before the first (1st) day of February, May, August and November in each Contract Year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Retainer Fee" as "Consideration" in the introductory sentence, while the schedule and subsequent sentences in the same paragraph continue to use "Retainer Fee." This directly contradicts the intention of the contract and creates confusion as to what "Consideration" actually entails, when "Retainer Fee" has already been defined and a schedule for it already exists. The Contradicted Text exhibits this very same issue. Therefore, there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: TPC shall pay any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a Major Tournament win within sixty (60) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage and any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a network televised US PGA Tour Tournament win shall be paid within forty-five (45) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage. All other Tournament Bonuses due to Consolidated Artists shall be paid by TPC within thirty (30) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage.
perturbation.changed_text: TPC shall pay any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a Major Tournament win within sixty (60) days following TPC's receipt of a statement from Consolidated Artists or Advantage and any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a network televised US PGA Tour Tournament win shall be paid within forty-five (45) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage. All other Tournament Bonuses due to Consolidated Artists shall be paid by TPC within thirty (30) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'invoice' to 'statement' for Major Tournament wins creates inconsistency. The subsequent sentences still refer to 'invoice' for other tournament bonuses. This introduces a contradiction on what type of document is needed to claim the payment on Major Tournament Wins.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TPC shall pay any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a Major Tournament win within sixty (60) days following TPC's receipt of a statement from Consolidated Artists or Advantage and any Tournament Bonuses due Consolidated Artists for a network televised US PGA Tour Tournament win shall be paid within forty-five (45) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage. All other Tournament Bonuses due to Consolidated Artists shall be paid by TPC within thirty (30) days following TPC's receipt of an invoice from Consolidated Artists or Advantage.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'invoice' to 'statement' for Major Tournament wins introduces a direct contradiction. The modified text states that a 'statement' is required for Major Tournament wins, while the subsequent sentences and the contradicted text state that an 'invoice' is required for other tournament bonuses, creating a clear inconsistency in what type of document is needed. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the instructions, directly leads to conflicting obligations, since it is unclear which document is required for a specific payment, causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the Contract Period, TPC shall supply Consolidated Artists, at no charge, with such quantities of the Endorsed Product as Consolidated Artists may reasonably request for Ogle's use and the personal use of Ogle's immediate family. In addition, TPC shall supply Consolidated Artists at normal wholesale price with such quantities of the Endorsed Product as Ogle may reasonably request for gifts to others.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Contract Period, TPC shall supply Consolidated Artists, at no charge, with such quantities of the Product as Consolidated Artists may reasonably request for Ogle's use and the personal use of Ogle's immediate family. In addition, TPC shall supply Consolidated Artists at normal wholesale price with such quantities of the Endorsed Product as Ogle may reasonably request for gifts to others.
perturbation.explanation: In paragraph 18, 'Endorsed Product' is changed to 'Product' in the first sentence but remains 'Endorsed Product' in the second. This creates uncertainty whether Ogle's family gets all products or just the endorsed ones, while gifts must be 'Endorsed Products'. The definition of 'Endorsed Product' from section 1(b) conflicts on which one to use here, creating an in-text contradiction and confusion with which product the family would be getting for personal use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Contract Period, TPC shall supply Consolidated Artists, at no charge, with such quantities of the Product as Consolidated Artists may reasonably request for Ogle's use and the personal use of Ogle's immediate family. In addition, TPC shall supply Consolidated Artists at normal wholesale price with such quantities of the Endorsed Product as Ogle may reasonably request for gifts to others.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the first sentence to refer to 'Product' instead of 'Endorsed Product' while retaining 'Endorsed Product' in the second sentence. This creates a direct conflict, as it's now unclear whether Ogle's family receives all 'Products' or just 'Endorsed Products' for personal use, directly contradicting the previously consistent use of 'Endorsed Product' throughout the paragraph. This creates an ambiguity as described in the explanation where the definition of Endorsed Product now conflicts with the use of Product, and therefore it is a strong in-text contradiction according to the provided explanation and the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
